```python
# test_snake_to_camel.py
import pytest

from your_module import snake_to_camel


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "inp, expected",
    [
        ("", ""),  # empty string
        ("already_camel", "alreadyCamel"),  # no underscores; treated as single part
        ("simple_test", "simpleTest"),
        ("two_parts", "twoParts"),
        ("leading_under_score", "leadingUnderScore"),
        ("trailing_under_score_", "trailingUnderScore"),  # trailing underscore => last part empty
        ("multiple___underscores", "multipleUnderscores"),  # consecutive underscores => empty parts removed by capitalize()
        ("__starts_with_two", "StartsWithTwo"),  # leading empty parts; first part empty => CamelCase starts immediately
        ("ends_with__double__", "endsWithDouble"),  # trailing consecutive underscores produce empty parts
    ],
)
def test_snake_to_camel_happy_paths_and_edge_cases(inp, expected):
    assert snake_to_camel(inp) == expected


def test_snake_to_camel_type_error_for_non_string():
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        # Non-string has no .split attribute (e.g., None)
        snake_to_camel(None)  # type: ignore[arg-type]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("inp", [123, 45.6, True, [], {}, object()])
def test_snake_to_camel_error_for_non_string_types(inp):
    # For numbers/bools/lists/dicts/objects, .split will fail (AttributeError).
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        snake_to_camel(inp)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
```
